title: generate_id uses Relaxed ordering in CAS
description: In smallhold src/id.rs lines 13 and 30, the ID generator uses Ordering::Relaxed for both the load and compare_exchange operations. While the CAS loop ensures monotonicity within a single thread, Relaxed ordering allows stale reads across threads. Two threads can race such that thread A generates ID X, thread B generates ID Y where Y < X, but thread B's database write commits first. This breaks the implied total ordering that consumers expect from snowflake-style IDs. The test at line 43 (ids_are_monotonic) only validates single-threaded monotonicity. Fix: use Ordering::AcqRel for the success case of compare_exchange and Ordering::Acquire for the load, ensuring cross-thread visibility.
